title: Child's Airplane Swing
summary: A tapered plywood-and-board airplane body, seat, and wings that hang from rope through eye bolts — a small, quick build with the safety hardware doing the real work.
description:
The body, tail, and wings are all just tapered and rounded board shapes — genuinely simple cuts, and easy to treat as the whole project. The part actually worth taking seriously is the suspension hardware: eye lag bolts with washers, rated for the load, threaded well into solid wood, and rope clips positioned beneath the upper eye bolts to keep the plane level. That hardware is what a child's full weight and swinging motion load into, not the plywood body.

Attach the propeller only after painting is finished, since it's a separate spinning piece and painting it in place would either glue it solid or make a mess of the paint job.

tools.note: Cuts the tapered and rounded body, tail, and wing shapes.
Bores the dowel-handle hole and the eye-bolt pilot holes.

cutList.note: Tapered toward the back, rounded at the front bottom.
Cut from scrap; drilled for the 1" dowel handle.

steps.title: Cut and assemble the sections
steps.body: Cut all the body, seat, tail, and wing pieces per the cut list, tapering and rounding as noted. Begin assembling the tail section, then the seat section, then the wing section, attaching each with 2" exterior screws and glue.

steps.title: Drill the handle bar and mount hardware
steps.body: Drill a hole sized to the 1" dowel in the handle bar scrap and glue the dowel in place. Drill pilot holes and install the eye screw and eye lag bolts at the marked suspension points, backed with washers for load-bearing strength.

steps.title: Sand and paint
steps.body: Sand every surface smooth, then paint. Attach the propeller with the lag screw and washers only after the paint has fully dried, so it can still spin freely.

steps.title: Rig the swing
steps.body: Feed the rope through the plane and secure with strong knots underneath the wings. Use rope clips beneath the upper eye bolts to keep the airplane hanging level.

images.alt: Child's wooden airplane swing hanging from rope
